summary: Teya's homepage has minimal structured data and lacks the schema markup, entity signals, and FAQ content that AI engines need to confidently cite it. While the page communicates core value propositions clearly in prose, the absence of Organization, Product, and FAQPage schema means AI engines cannot reliably extract and attribute key facts about Teya's identity, pricing, or geographic availability. Adding targeted JSON-LD and FAQ blocks would materially improve Teya's chances of being surfaced in AI-generated answers about payment solutions for small businesses.

entity_signals.org_identity: Weak. The page names the company as Teya and states it has been empowering small local businesses since 2019, but provides no registered company name, company number, headquarters address, or founding narrative that AI engines can anchor to.
entity_signals.sameAs_links: Absent. No links to LinkedIn, Crunchbase, Companies House, Wikipedia, or other authoritative external profiles are present in the scraped content, making cross-platform entity resolution difficult for AI models.
entity_signals.authority_markers: Partial. Trust signals exist (Trustpilot rating of 4.3/5 from 1,259 reviews, 75,000+ active members, £68M+ in Cash Advance issued, 609M+ transactions in 2025) but none are formalised in structured data where AI engines can reliably parse and cite them.

question_coverage.question: What is Teya?
question_coverage.note: The headline and subheadline describe Teya as an integrated payments, funding and Business Account solution for local businesses founded in 2019, but there is no concise definitional paragraph an AI engine can cleanly extract as a single citable answer.

question_coverage.question: What payment solutions does Teya offer?
question_coverage.note: Products like card machines, Tap to Pay, Pay by Link, split bills, and EPOS integrations are mentioned across the page but are scattered in feature tiles rather than consolidated in a scannable, citable list.

question_coverage.question: How does Teya help small businesses?
question_coverage.note: Multiple features (next-day settlements, cash advance, real-time sales tracking, lifetime warranty, human support) and customer testimonials directly address this, giving AI engines reasonable citation material.

question_coverage.question: What are Teya's fees?
question_coverage.note: The Start plan rate of 1.59% for consumer cards and a £29.99 monthly fee for turnover under £2,500 are mentioned, but the full fee schedule is truncated and not structured in a way AI engines can easily parse.

question_coverage.question: Is Teya available in my country?
question_coverage.note: No geographic availability information, list of supported countries, or regulatory context is present anywhere in the scraped content.

question_coverage.question: How do I sign up for Teya?
question_coverage.note: There is a 'Join Us' CTA and a pricing link but no step-by-step onboarding explanation, required documents, or sign-up process description that an AI engine could summarise.

json_ld_additions.gap: Organisation identity, authority markers, and sameAs entity links are entirely absent, preventing AI engines from reliably identifying and citing Teya as a known entity.

code.name: Teya
code.description: Teya is a financial technology company founded in 2019 that provides integrated payment solutions, business accounts, and funding products to small and local businesses. Its offerings include card machines, Tap to Pay, Pay by Link, Cash Advance, and a Business Debit Account with 0.5% cashback.

json_ld_additions.gap: Top user questions about Teya's identity, fees, geographic availability, and sign-up process are not covered in any machine-readable FAQ format, reducing AI citation probability for high-intent queries.

acceptedAnswer.text: Teya is a financial technology company founded in 2019 that offers integrated payment, funding, and business account solutions designed specifically for small and local businesses. Its platform combines card machines, Tap to Pay, Pay by Link, real-time sales analytics, Cash Advance funding, and a Business Debit Account with 0.5% cashback — all in a single ecosystem. Teya serves over 75,000 active business members and has processed more than 609 million transactions in 2025.

Teya uses a membership plan structure based on monthly card turnover. The Start plan, for businesses with up to £7,500 in monthly card turnover, charges 1.59% on consumer card transactions. A £29.99 monthly fee applies when turnover falls below £2,500. Teya advertises simple, transparent rates with no hidden fees. Businesses with higher turnover can contact Teya's sales team for a custom plan.

mainEntity.name: How do I sign up for Teya?

acceptedAnswer.text: To sign up for Teya, visit teya.com/pricing to select a membership plan that matches your monthly card turnover. You can choose a card machine such as the Teya Pro, complete the online application, and benefit from a quick set-up process. Teya offers next-day settlements and human support to help new members get started. For high-volume businesses, a specialist consultation is available via teya.com/contact-sales.

mainEntity.name: What payment solutions does Teya offer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Teya offers a comprehensive suite of payment solutions including countertop and portable card machines, Teya Tap (turning a smartphone into a card reader), Pay by Link for remote payments, split bill functionality, and 50+ EPOS integrations. Payments can be settled the next business day or instantly (subject to eligibility). All payment activity is tracked in real-time through the Teya App and Web Portal.

mainEntity.name: Is Teya available in my country?

acceptedAnswer.text: Teya currently operates in the United Kingdom, with its customer support team based in the UK. The company serves over 75,000 small and local businesses. For information on availability in other countries, prospective customers should contact Teya directly at teya.com.

mainEntity.name: How does Teya help small businesses?

acceptedAnswer.text: Teya helps small businesses by consolidating payments, funding, and expense management into one platform. Key benefits include next-day or instant settlements for improved cash flow, Cash Advance funding (with over £68 million issued to date) repaid flexibly from card sales, a Business Debit Account with 0.5% cashback, real-time sales tracking, 50+ EPOS integrations, lifetime warranty on card machines, and UK-based human support available when needed.

json_ld_additions.gap: Teya's core financial products (card machines, Business Account, Cash Advance) lack Product or FinancialProduct schema, meaning AI engines cannot surface structured product details, pricing, or feature comparisons when users ask about specific Teya offerings.

code.name: Teya Products and Services
code.description: Teya's integrated suite of payment, funding, and business account products for small businesses.

item.name: Teya Card Machines
item.description: Physical payment terminals including the Teya Pro, designed for fast in-person card payments with industry-leading transaction speeds, split bill functionality, built-in calculator, tipping, and lifetime warranty covering theft and water damage.

priceSpecification.description: Teya Pro card machine, plus VAT. Transaction rate from 1.59% for consumer cards on the Start plan.

item.name: Teya Cash Advance
item.description: Flexible business funding product where repayments are taken automatically as a percentage of daily card machine sales. Over £68 million has been advanced to Teya's small business customers to date.
item.feesAndCommissionsSpecification: Repayments are deducted directly from card machine sales; no fixed monthly repayment required.

item.name: Teya Business Account
item.description: A business debit account that integrates directly with Teya's payment terminals, allowing users to create multiple cards with individual spending limits and earn 0.5% cashback on all business spending.
item.feesAndCommissionsSpecification: 0.5% cashback on all card spending. Included within Teya membership plans.

faq_blocks.question: Is Teya available in my country?
faq_blocks.answer: Teya currently operates in the United Kingdom, where its customer support team is also based. The platform serves over 75,000 small and local businesses across the UK. Teya's pricing, card machines, and Business Account are structured around GBP transactions and UK regulatory requirements. Businesses outside the UK should contact Teya directly at teya.com to enquire about availability in other markets.

faq_blocks.question: How do I sign up for Teya?
faq_blocks.answer: Signing up for Teya starts at teya.com/pricing, where you select a membership plan based on your monthly card turnover. You then choose a card machine — such as the Teya Pro at £179 plus VAT — and complete the online registration. Teya is designed for quick set-up, and new members receive access to next-day settlements, the Teya App for real-time sales tracking, and UK-based human support from day one. Businesses with higher turnover can request a custom plan by speaking with a specialist.

faq_blocks.question: What are Teya's fees?
faq_blocks.answer: Teya's fees are structured around membership plans tied to monthly card turnover. The Start plan (up to £7,500/month in card turnover) charges 1.59% on consumer card transactions. A £29.99 monthly fee applies if turnover falls below £2,500 in a given month. Teya advertises no hidden fees and simple, transparent rates. Businesses with turnover exceeding the standard plan thresholds can contact Teya's sales team for a tailored pricing arrangement.
